name: Holy Diver
content:
I was intending to go to a different bar the night when I went to Holy Diver, but ended up taking a wrong turn when this place caught my eye and I decided I would stop to check it out. There was a show here to celebrate the birthday of a former bartender. The vibes were very local and very friendly along the whole street where Holy Diver is located. I spent the night chatting away with random people at the bar and along the street, with everyone just floating in between various conversations and no clear line on who was or wasn’t at the bar. They have a pretty cool pool room as well. This place was a lot of fun Unlike my travel mates who stayed home to watch TV.. I would recommend stopping by any of the bars in the vicinity of Holy Diver.
The reason I was looking for a bar alone instead of with my travel companions was that all of them had decided that they wanted to stay home that night and watch TV, despite the fact that we had driven all the way out to New Orleans from Tuscaloosa to celebrate Independence Day. Having become fed up with this attitude, I figured it was time I went to a proper gay bar instead of my usual go-to of frequenting the indie/alt bars that double as the queer hangout area in small towns. After some searching on the Internet, I found out that most of the gay bars in New Orleans were bear bars I would have been half the age of their youngest patron., received most of their patronage from middle-age white married straight women A perfectly alright demographic as long as they don’t fetishize anyone, for example by hanging out at a bear bar., and were currently under boycott by most of the New Orleans queer community anyway Something to do with the fact that they were all owned by the same guy, and the queer community Did Not Like That Guy. I didn’t quite have the time to figure out why, but if I wanted to find some people to hang out with, it would be hard to do that at a place being boycotted by the people I wanted to hang out with.. Holy Diver definitely is not a gay bar, but fits more into a category of cozy local hang out bar that might as well be your pal’s living room
